title: Gravity, Scroll, Proximity, Glare & Accordion
description: Gravity mouse trail, sticky scroll reveal, variable-font proximity grid, CSS glare sweep, and a zero-JS animated accordion.

drops.title: Gravity Image Mouse Trail
drops.description: Objects spawn at the cursor, fall with elastic gravity, bounce on landing, then vanish — Web Animations API.
drops.concept: On mousemove, the cursor's travel distance is accumulated; every ~85px a new emoji element spawns at the cursor position. It's animated through three Web Animations API phases chained via .finished promises: a scale-in + gravity fall (cubic-bezier easing), a squash-bounce landing, and a fade-out shrink. No external libraries — all native browser animation primitives.

interaction.click: Tap/click to burst 4 objects at once (mobile-friendly)

drops.title: Sticky Scroll Grid Reveal
drops.description: A sticky 3-column grid drives three phases as you scroll: column reveal, zoom-expand, and content settle.
drops.concept: A tall scroll wrapper (420vh) contains a sticky inner div. As the user scrolls, JS maps scroll progress (0–1) to three phases: columns slide in from top/bottom (0–0.4), the grid scales up 2.2× and columns drift apart (0.4–0.88), then a title text fades in (0.88–1). All driven by smoothstep easing and vanilla scroll events — no GSAP, no CSS scroll-driven animations.
drops.source: Theo Plawinski / Codrops Sticky Grid Scroll (March 2, 2026)

interaction.mouse: Scroll down to drive the three-phase animation

drops.title: Proximity Variable-Font Grid
drops.description: A full-viewport grid of characters pulses font-weight and color based on real-time cursor distance.
drops.concept: A dense grid of individual <span> elements is rendered with random characters. On each animation frame, the Euclidean distance from each span's center to the cursor is computed. Within a configurable radius, font-weight is interpolated from 200 to 900 and color from dark blue to bright purple, creating a radial weight gradient that follows the mouse. Inspired by the Exat microsite's variable font proximity technique documented on Codrops.

interaction.mouse: Move cursor to attract character weight toward it
interaction.touch: Touch-move also drives the proximity effect
interaction.keyboard: Radius and max-weight sliders for tuning

drops.title: Glare Hover Sweep
drops.description: A diagonal light-glare gradient sweeps once across any card or button on hover — pure CSS, zero JS.
drops.concept: A ::before pseudo-element with a diagonal linear-gradient (transparent→white rgba→transparent) sits at background-position: -100% -100% (offscreen). On :hover, background-position transitions to 200% 200% over 700ms ease-out — sweeping the glare diagonally across the surface in a single, satisfying pass. The transition resets instantly on mouse-leave so the next hover triggers a fresh sweep. Popularized by the Magic UI component library.
drops.source: Magic UI / Dillion Verma — glare-hover component (2026)

interaction.mouse: Hover over any card or button to trigger the glare sweep

drops.title: Native Details Accordion
drops.description: CSS-only animated accordion: <details name> for exclusivity + interpolate-size for height:auto animation.
drops.concept: Three native HTML/CSS features combine for a zero-JS animated accordion: (1) the <details name> attribute creates a browser-native exclusive group where opening one item closes others; (2) interpolate-size: allow-keywords on :root enables transitioning height: 0 → height: auto; (3) ::details-content pseudo-element targets only the non-summary content, allowing bidirectional height transitions. The +/× icon uses a spring cubic-bezier rotate transition for a satisfying bounce.

interaction.mouse: Click any summary row to open/close with animated height
